text: The surgeon reduces a small bowel from an incarcerated indirect inguinal hernia, working from medial to lateral towards Bogros' space. The surgical field shows thin tissue manipulation with touch cautery, pushing nerves laterally and pulling down the flap.

The surgeon transitions from the flap to the supravesicular space, extending medially beyond the symphysis pubis. The infrared epigastric vessels are visible, with the indirect inguinal hernia located laterally. The surgeon sweeps fat laterally to cover the nerves, using touch cautery to separate tissue, while applying traction with a Maryland dissector down and to the left and right.

The surgeon uses cautery on the fat while dissecting down into the hernia sac, pulling it up and to the left. The indirect hernia sac is tugged and pulled down at an oblique angle, with vessels and fat swept laterally. A cord lipoma becomes visible as it is released and pulled back inside.

The surgeon sweeps tissue down, revealing the vas at the ten o'clock position, while working laterally to create a flap. The lateral fascia is swept to cover nerves, preparing for mesh placement. The large hernia extends to the deep internal ring and crosses medially. The surgeon carefully maneuvers around the vas and deferential vessels to visualize the iliac vein.

The surgeon carefully dissects around the medial portion near the deep internal ring, teasing the tissue until it drops, while ensuring the flap is large enough to accommodate the mesh without buckling. The iliac vein is visible in the background as the surgeon continues to secure the flap.

The surgeon places two sutures for high lateral fixation near the coronamortis distal vein, ensuring the muscle is visible to prevent blind suturing. The mesh is secured to minimize migration and potential patient discomfort, using a standard one-hand technique to close the area.

The surgeon sutures the hernia sac back onto the flap, ensuring there are no loops that could lead to bowel obstruction or internal hernia. The suturing technique involves a purse-string method to close any defects and prevent recurrence or entanglement with intestinal structures.
